message:
test(metamorphic): freeze concrete output snapshots in golden_metamorphic_search harness

The existing tests/metamorphic_search.rs asserts invariants
(idempotence, limit prefix, agent union, days subset, case
invariance, agent exclusivity) on a deterministic 3-agent ×
5-conversation × 3-message corpus. Those invariants catch a lot,
but they don't catch silent ranking / tokenization drifts that
preserve every invariant while changing *which* hits come out in
*what* order — e.g. a tokenizer change that re-ranks a subset,
or a scoring tweak that reshuffles ties.

tests/golden_metamorphic_search.rs

- New golden harness, keyed to the *same* deterministic corpus
  as metamorphic_search.rs. Seeds the identical TantivyIndex
  (3 agents × 5 conversations × 3 messages) at the same fixed
  timestamp (1_768_435_200_000 — 2026-01-15 UTC) so the goldens
  are byte-for-byte reproducible across runs, machines, and
  agents.
- The header doc comment spells out the regeneration command
  (`UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_metamorphic_search`)
  and review instruction (`git diff tests/golden/metamorphic/`)
  to keep the workflow obvious for the next reader.
- Corpus construction is intentionally duplicated from
  metamorphic_search.rs rather than extracted. The comment
  on `AGENTS` explicitly calls out that the corpora MUST match;
  factoring it into a shared util would create a footgun where
  editing one file silently invalidates goldens keyed to the
  other, and goldens shouldn't be a moving target.

tests/metamorphic_search.rs

- Pure `cargo fmt` / struct-init-style cleanup touched on the
  way through: long format! calls collapsed to one-line format,
  `let mut filters = SearchFilters::default(); filters.created_
  from = Some(...)` rewritten as struct-update syntax
  (`SearchFilters { created_from: Some(...), ..Default::default()
  }`), multi-line `.search(lower, SearchFilters::default(),
  limit, 0, FieldMask::FULL)` calls put on one line. No
  behavioral change — same queries, same filters, same
  assertions. This is just the file being reformatted against
  the current rustfmt target so the new golden harness (which
  references the same corpus and helper names) lands on a clean
  baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
